figures.caption: Table 3 One-dimensional accuracy test-I: 𝐿1 errors and order of accuracy for 𝜌.
Table 4 One-dimensional accuracy test-II: 𝐿1-errors and order of accuracy for 𝑝𝑒 and 𝑝𝑖.
Figure 1: Double rarefaction Riemann problem: Plots of density, electron pressure, and ion pressure at 𝑡 = 2.0 using 2000 cells. We have also plotted total entropy change with time.
Figure 2: Sod Riemann problem: Plots of density, electron pressure, and ion pressure at 𝑡 = 2.0 using 2000 cells. We have also plotted total entropy change with time.
Figure 3: Lax Riemann problem: Plots of density, electron pressure, and ion pressure at 𝑡 = 2.0 using 2000 cells. We have also plotted total entropy change with time.
Figure 4: Two-dimensional Riemann problem: Plots of density, electron pressure, and ion pressure at time 𝑡 = 0.75 using 400 × 400 cells for 𝛾𝑒 = 𝛾𝑖 = 1.4.
Figure 5: Two-dimensional Riemann problem: Plots of density, electron pressure, and ion pressure at time 𝑡 = 0.59 using 400 × 400 cells for 𝛾𝑒 = 1.4, 𝛾𝑖 = 1.67.
Figure 6: Two-dimensional Riemann problem: Plots of total entropy change with time using 400 × 400 cells for both the cases using O2es exp, O3es exp and O4es exp schemes.
Figure 7: Shock-bubble interaction problem: Plots of density and total entropy evolution at time 𝑡 = 7.1571 using 400×144 cells. We have also plotted the total entropy change with time.
Figure 8: Shock-bubble interaction problem: Plots of density for O4es exp scheme using 400 × 144 cells at different times.
Figure 9: Richtmyer-Meshkov instability: Plots of density, electron pressure and ion pressure at time 𝑡 = 17.46 using 800 × 800 cells.
Figure 10: Richtmyer-Meshkov instability: Zoomed plots of density, electron pressure and ion pressure at time 𝑡 = 17.46 using 800 × 800 cells.
Figure 11: Richtmyer-Meshkov instability: Plots of total entropy change with time using 800 × 800 cells for O2es exp, O3es exp and O4es exp schemes.
